name: django-compressor

metadata.name: django-compressor
metadata.summary: ('Compresses linked and inline JavaScript or CSS into single cached files.',)

documentation:
# Django Compressor

```{image} https://codecov.io/github/django-compressor/django-compressor/coverage.svg?branch=develop
:target: https://codecov.io/github/django-compressor/django-compressor?branch=develop
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/django_compressor.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/django_compressor
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/django-compressor/django-compressor/ci.yml?branch=develop
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor/actions?query=workflow%3ACI
```

Django Compressor processes, combines and minifies linked and inline
Javascript or CSS in a Django template into cacheable static files.

It supports compilers such as coffeescript, LESS and SASS and is
extensible by custom processing steps.

## How it works

In your templates, all HTML code between the tags `{% compress js/css %}` and
`{% endcompress %}` is parsed and searched for CSS or JS. These styles and
scripts are subsequently processed with optional, configurable compilers and
filters.

The default filter for CSS rewrites paths to static files to be absolute.
Both Javascript and CSS files are by default concatenated and minified.

As the final step the template tag outputs a `<script>` or `<link>`
tag pointing to the optimized file. Alternatively it can also
inline the resulting content into the original template directly.

Since the file name is dependent on the content, these files can be given
a far future expiration date without worrying about stale browser caches.

For increased performance, the concatenation and compressing process
can also be run once manually outside of the request/response cycle by using
the Django management command `manage.py compress`.

## Configurability & Extensibility

Django Compressor is highly configurable and extensible. The HTML parsing
is done using [lxml] or if it's not available Python's built-in HTMLParser by
default. As an alternative Django Compressor provides a [BeautifulSoup] and a
[html5lib] based parser, as well as an abstract base class that makes it easy to
write a custom parser.

Django Compressor also comes with built-in support for
[YUI CSS and JS] compressor, [yUglify CSS and JS] compressor, Google's
[Closure Compiler], a Python port of Douglas Crockford's [JSmin], a Python port
of the YUI CSS Compressor [csscompressor] and a filter to convert (some) images into
[data URIs].

If your setup requires a different compressor or other post-processing
tool it will be fairly easy to implement a custom filter. Simply extend
from one of the available base classes.

More documentation about the usage and settings of Django Compressor can be
found on [django-compressor.readthedocs.org].

The source code for Django Compressor can be found and contributed to on
[github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor]. There you can also file tickets.

The in-development version of Django Compressor can be installed with
`pip install git+https://github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor.git`

[beautifulsoup]: http://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/
[closure compiler]: http://code.google.com/closure/compiler/
[csscompressor]: https://github.com/sprymix/csscompressor
[data uris]: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Data_URI_scheme
[django-compressor.readthedocs.org]: https://django-compressor.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
[github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor]: https://github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor
[html5lib]: https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python
[jsmin]: http://www.crockford.com/javascript/jsmin.html
[lxml]: http://lxml.de/
[yuglify css and js]: https://github.com/yui/yuglify
[yui css and js]: http://developer.yahoo.com/yui/compressor/

methods.name: compressor.filters.css_default.CssAbsoluteFilter.post_process_url
documentation: Extra URL processing, to be overridden in subclasses.

classes.name: compressor.filters.css_default.CssRelativeFilter

methods.name: compressor.filters.css_default.CssRelativeFilter.post_process_url
documentation:
Replace `settings.COMPRESS_URL` URL prefix with  '../' * (N + 1)
where N is the *depth* of `settings.COMPRESS_OUTPUT_DIR` folder.

E.g. by default `settings.COMPRESS_OUTPUT_DIR == 'CACHE'`,
the depth is 1, and the prefix will be '../../'.

If `settings.COMPRESS_OUTPUT_DIR == 'my/compiled/data'`,
the depth is 3, and the prefix will be '../../../../'.

Example:

- original file URL: '/static/my-app/style.css'
- it has an image link: `url(images/logo.svg)`
- compiled file URL: '/static/CACHE/css/output.abcdef123456.css'
- replaced image link URL: `url(../../my-app/images/logo.svg)`

Do similar to `CssAbsoluteFilter` URL processing
but add a *relative URL prefix* instead of `settings.COMPRESS_URL`.

modules.name: compressor.filters
documentation: flake8: noqa

classes.name: compressor.filters.datauri.DataUriFilter
documentation:
Filter for embedding media as data: URIs.

Settings:
     COMPRESS_DATA_URI_MAX_SIZE: Only files that are smaller than this
                                 value will be embedded. Unit; bytes.


Don't use this class directly. Use a subclass.

classes.name: compressor.filters.datauri.CssDataUriFilter
documentation:
Filter for embedding media as data: URIs in CSS files.

See DataUriFilter.

classes.name: compressor.filters.cssmin.CSSCompressorFilter
documentation:
A filter that utilizes Yury Selivanov's Python port of the YUI CSS
compression algorithm: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/csscompressor

methods.name: compressor.js.JsCompressor.filter_input
documentation:
Passes each hunk (file or code) to the 'input' methods
of the compressor filters.

classes.name: compressor.filters.base.FilterBase
documentation:
A base class for filters that does nothing.

Subclasses should implement `input` and/or `output` methods which must
return a string or raise a NotImplementedError.

classes.name: compressor.filters.base.CallbackOutputFilter
documentation:
A filter which takes function path in `callback` attribute, imports it
and uses that function to filter output string:

```
class MyFilter(CallbackOutputFilter):
    callback = 'path.to.my.callback'
```

Callback should be a function which takes a string as first argument and
returns a string.

classes.name: compressor.filters.base.CompilerFilter
documentation:
A filter subclass that is able to filter content via
external commands.

functions.name: compressor.templatetags.compress.compress
documentation:
Compresses linked and inline javascript or CSS into a single cached file.

Syntax:

```
{% compress <js/css> [<file/inline> [block_name]] %}
<html of inline or linked JS/CSS>
{% endcompress %}
```

Examples:

```
See docs/usage.rst
```

methods.name: compressor.templatetags.compress.CompressorMixin.is_offline_compression_enabled
documentation:
Check if offline compression is enabled or forced

Defaults to just checking the settings and forced argument,
but can be overridden to completely disable compression for
a subclass, for instance.

methods.name: compressor.templatetags.compress.CompressorMixin.render_offline
documentation:
If enabled and in offline mode, and not forced check the offline cache
and return the result if given

methods.name: compressor.templatetags.compress.CompressorMixin.render_cached
documentation:
If enabled checks the cache for the given compressor's cache key
and return a tuple of cache key and output

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_storages.DummyPathNotImplementedStorage

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_storages.CompressorFileNameTestCase.test_storage_without_path_fallback

Remote storages not implementing path need a fallback to a private
instance of CompressorFileStorage. This must not be dependent on

functions.name: compressor.tests.test_templatetags.script
documentation:
returns a unicode text html script element.

```pycon
>>> script('#this is a comment', scripttype="text/applescript")
'<script type="text/applescript">#this is a comment</script>'
```

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_jinja2ext.TestJinja2CompressorExtension
documentation:
Test case for jinja2 extension.

:::{note}
At tests we need to make some extra care about whitespace. Please note
that we use jinja2 specific controls (*minus* character at block's
beginning or end). For more information see jinja2 documentation.
:::

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_filters.SpecializedFiltersTest
documentation: Test to check the Specializations of filters.

functions.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.script_prefix
documentation:
Override `SCRIPT_NAME` WSGI param, yield, then restore its original value.

```
param new_prefix

New SCRIPT_NAME value.
```

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.LazyScriptNamePrefixedUrl

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.LazyScriptNamePrefixedUrl.lstrip
documentation: Override `.lstrip()` method to make it work with `{% static %}`.

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.LazyScriptNamePrefixedUrl.split
documentation: Override `.split()` method to make it work with `{% static %}`.

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.LazyScriptNamePrefixedUrl.replace
documentation:
Override `.replace()` to make it work with `{% static %}`.

In `django.core.files.storage`, `FileSystemStorage.url()` passes
this object to `urllib.parse.urljoin`.

In `urllib.parse`, the function that calls `replace()` is
`_remove_unsafe_bytes_from_url()`.

Lazy URL with `SCRIPT_NAME` WSGI param as path prefix.

```python
settings.STATIC_URL = LazyScriptNamePrefixedUrl('/static/')

# HTTP request to '/some/page/' without SCRIPT_NAME
str(settings.STATIC_URL) == '/static/'

# HTTP request to '/app/prefix/some/page/` with SCRIPT_NAME = '/app/prefix/'
str(settings.STATIC_URL) == '/app/prefix/static/'

# HTTP request to '/another/prefix/some/page/` with SCRIPT_NAME = '/another/prefix/'
str(settings.STATIC_URL) == '/another/prefix/static/'
```

The implementation is incomplete, all `str` methods must be overridden
in order to work correctly with the rest of Django core.

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressStaticUrlIndependenceTestCase
documentation:
Test that the offline manifest is independent of STATIC_URL.
I.e. users can use the manifest with any other STATIC_URL in the future.

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressTestCaseWithError._test_offline
documentation:
Test that a CommandError is raised with DEBUG being False as well as
True, as otherwise errors in configuration will never show in
production.

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressEmptyTag
documentation:
In case of a compress template tag with no content, an entry
will be added to the manifest with an empty string as value.
This test makes sure there is no recompression happening when
compressor encounters such an emptystring in the manifest.

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressExtendsRecursionTestCase
documentation:
Test that templates extending templates with the same name
(e.g. admin/index.html) don't cause an infinite test_extends_recursion

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressExtendsRelativeTestCase
documentation:
Test that templates extending templates using relative paths
(e.g. ./base.html) are evaluated correctly

classes.name: compressor.tests.test_offline.OfflineCompressTestCaseWithLazyStringAlikeUrls
documentation:
Test offline compressing with `STATIC_URL` and `COMPRESS_URL` as instances of
*lazy string-alike objects* instead of strings.

In particular, lazy string-alike objects that add `SCRIPT_NAME` WSGI param
as URL path prefix.

For example:

- We've generated offline assets and deployed them with our Django project.
- We've configured HTTP server (e.g. Nginx) to serve our app at two different URLs:
  `http://example.com/my/app/` and `http://app.example.com/`.
- Both URLs are leading to the same app, but in the first case we pass
  `SCRIPT_NAME = /my/app/` to WSGI app server (e.g. to uWSGI, which is *behind* Nginx).
- Django (1.11.7, as of today) *ignores* `SCRIPT_NAME` when generating
  static URLs, while it uses `SCRIPT_NAME` when generating Django views URLs -
  see <https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/25598>.
- As a solution - we can use a lazy string-alike object instead of `str` for `STATIC_URL`
  so it will know about `SCRIPT_NAME` and add it as a prefix every time we do any
  string operation with `STATIC_URL`.
- However, there are some cases when we cannot force CPython to render our lazy string
  correctly - e.g. `some_string.replace(STATIC_URL, '...')`. So we need to do explicit
  `str` type cast: `some_string.replace(str(STATIC_URL), '...')`.

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_parsers.Html5LibParserTests.test_css_split

methods.name: compressor.tests.test_parsers.BeautifulSoupParserTests.test_css_split

Basically passes the string through the {% compress 'js' %} template tag

classes.name: compressor.finders.CompressorFinder
documentation:
A staticfiles finder that looks in COMPRESS_ROOT
for compressed files, to be used during development
with staticfiles development file server or during
deployment.

classes.name: compressor.storage.CompressorFileStorage
documentation:
Standard file system storage for files handled by django-compressor.

The defaults for `location` and `base_url` are `COMPRESS_ROOT` and
`COMPRESS_URL`.

classes.name: compressor.storage.GzipCompressorFileStorage
documentation: File system storage that stores gzipped files in addition to the usual files.

classes.name: compressor.storage.BrotliCompressorFileStorage
documentation: File system storage that stores brotli files in addition to the usual files.

classes.name: compressor.base.Compressor

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.split_contents
documentation:
To be implemented in a subclass, should return an
iterable with four values: kind, value, basename, element

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.get_template_name
documentation: Returns the template path for the given mode.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.get_basename
documentation:
Takes full path to a static file (eg. "/static/css/style.css") and
returns path with storage's base url removed (eg. "css/style.css").

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.get_filepath
documentation:
Returns file path for an output file based on contents.

Returned path is relative to compressor storage's base url, for
example "CACHE/css/58a8c0714e59.css".

When `basename` argument is provided then file name (without extension)
will be used as a part of returned file name, for example:

get_filepath(content, "my_file.css") -> 'CACHE/css/my_file.58a8c0714e59.css'

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.get_filename
documentation:
Returns full path to a file, for example:

get_filename('css/one.css') -> '/full/path/to/static/css/one.css'

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.get_filecontent
documentation: Reads file contents using given `charset` and returns it as text.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.hunks
documentation:
The heart of content parsing, iterates over the
list of split contents and looks at its kind
to decide what to do with it. Should yield a
bunch of precompiled and/or rendered hunks.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.filter_output
documentation:
Passes the concatenated content to the 'output' methods
of the compressor filters.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.filter_input
documentation:
Passes each hunk (file or code) to the 'input' methods
of the compressor filters.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.precompile
documentation:
Processes file using a pre compiler.

This is the place where files like coffee script are processed.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.output
documentation:
The general output method, override in subclass if you need to do
any custom modification. Calls other mode specific methods or simply
returns the content directly.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.output_file
documentation:
The output method that saves the content to a file and renders
the appropriate template with the file's URL.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.output_inline
documentation:
The output method that directly returns the content for inline
display.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.output_preload
documentation:
The output method that returns <link> with rel="preload" and
proper href attribute for given file.

methods.name: compressor.base.Compressor.render_output
documentation:
Renders the compressor output with the appropriate template for
the given mode and template context.

Base compressor object to be subclassed for content type
depending implementations details.

methods.name: compressor.management.commands.mtime_cache.Command.is_ignored

Return True or False depending on whether the `path` should be
ignored (if it matches any pattern in `ignore_patterns`).

modules.name: compressor.management.commands.compress
documentation: flake8: noqa

methods.name: compressor.management.commands.compress.Command.compress
documentation:
Searches templates containing 'compress' nodes and compresses them
"offline" -- outside of the request/response cycle.

The result is cached with a cache-key derived from the content of the
compress nodes (not the content of the possibly linked files!).

methods.name: compressor.management.commands.compress.Command.handle_extensions
documentation:
organizes multiple extensions that are separated with commas or
passed by using --extension/-e multiple times.

for example: running 'django-admin compress -e js,txt -e xhtml -a'
would result in an extension list: \['.js', '.txt', '.xhtml'\]

```pycon
>>> handle_extensions(['.html', 'html,js,py,py,py,.py', 'py,.py'])
['.html', '.js']
>>> handle_extensions(['.html, txt,.tpl'])
['.html', '.tpl', '.txt']
```
